Returns setting definitions and metadata used by Cloud Policy policy authoring and filtering.

Settings catalog filter expression. Observed example:
`Not(tag=Cloud Policies Composite),All(tag=Cloud Policies)`.

Settings catalog filter expression. Observed example:
`Not(tag=Cloud Policies Composite),All(tag=Cloud Policies)`.

Hidden auth settings catalog confirmed during the feature-gated probe
pass. The current tenant returned the same settings catalog entry shape as
the main Cloud Policy catalog.

The M365 Apps Config API is used by the Microsoft 365 Apps admin center
(`config.office.com`) for cloud update, servicing profiles, policy management,
device configuration, rollout metadata, and portal bootstrap state.

This surface was observed while browsing the authenticated M365 Apps admin
center pages for:

  - Home and Cloud Update
  - Device Configuration
  - Policy Management
  - Security Update Status
  - Setup

A second pass with token-backed probes confirmed additional servicing-profile
and settings catalog surfaces such as `serviceProfile/v3.0/profiles`,
`serviceProfile/v1.0/tenantrules`,
`serviceProfile/v1.0/ServiceProfileAggregator/tenantServicingExclusionWindows`,
`serviceProfile/v1.0/serviceProfileAggregator/filteredDevicesAndGroups`, and
`settings/v1.0/SettingsCatalog/auth/Settings`. Intercepted save flows also
exposed the paired `UpdateConfiguration` PUT requests emitted by the Office
Customization Tool editor.

The operations below were confirmed either by direct portal traffic,
token-backed probing with the same browser session, or by intercepting live
save requests before persistence.

Requests are issued with browser-obtained bearer tokens and commonly include
portal correlation headers such as `x-api-name`, `x-correlationid`,
`x-manageoffice-client-sid`, `x-requested-with`, and `x-start-time`.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best-efforts basis and
may not be 100% accurate.
